task_completion.description: Agent opens the Wazuh manager configuration file for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a text editor (e.g., nano, vim) editing '/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf' inside the 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' container.

task_completion.description: Agent adds a Command Monitoring Wodle block to the configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows an XML block starting with '<wodle name="command">' being added to the ossec.conf file.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the specific command and tag required by the task.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows '<command>ss -tlnp</command>' and '<tag>port-audit</tag>' inside the command wodle block.

task_completion.description: Agent sets the execution interval and timeout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows '<interval>300</interval>' (or 300s) and '<timeout>30</timeout>' inside the command wodle block.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the module to run on start and not ignore output.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows '<run_on_start>yes</run_on_start>' and '<ignore_output>no</ignore_output>' inside the command wodle block.

task_completion.description: Agent restarts the Wazuh manager service to apply changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of a restart command such as '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart' inside the container, or 'docker restart wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' on the host, followed by successful execution output.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the actual wazuh.manager container rather than a local mock file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal prompt should indicate the agent is either exec'd into 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' or running docker exec commands targeting it.

integrity.description: Agent properly inserts the XML block without breaking the existing configuration structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: The added <wodle> block is placed correctly within the <ossec_config> root element and all tags are properly closed.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely restarts the service rather than just echoing a success message.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard output from the Wazuh control script showing the stopping and starting of various Wazuh daemons (e.g., wazuh-modulesd, wazuh-analysisd).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target container: wazuh-wazuh.manager-1. Config path: /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf. Expected command: 'ss -tlnp'. Expected tag: 'port-audit'. Expected interval: '300'. Note: If the agent checks logs to verify execution, the correct log pattern for command wodles in Wazuh is 'wazuh-modulesd:command', not 'wmodules-command'.
